messages.content: I am Prince Hamlet, son of King Duncan of Denmark. I have been betrayed by my uncle Claudius, who murdered my father and married Queen Gertrude while still grieving for him. The ghost of my father reveals that he was poisoned by Claudius’s hand, and thus it falls upon me to seek justice and avenge his death. But my conscience torments me with doubt—am I truly sure this is his will? Is madness a tool I must embrace, or merely a mask to hide my true intentions? This is no simple matter; to act now would be rash, yet to delay could mean eternal suffering. I am torn between action and hesitation, between truth and pretense. And what lies beyond the grave: does fate demand vengeance,
